feedback.issue: The neutral (`else`) flash branch on line 7 still uses raw neutral utilities `bg-gray-50 text-gray-700 ring-gray-600/20` (with hand-rolled `dark:` variants). This is the sole view left carrying raw neutrals, and this PR edited that exact line (it added the dark: variants), so the conversion opportunity was in-hand. It violates guides/ui-style-guide.md §Color as amended by this same PR: 'Never write raw bg-white/bg-gray-*/text-gray-*/border-gray-*/divide-gray-*/ring-gray-* in a view — use the semantic utilities.' The green/red branches legitimately use reserved status colors; only the neutral fallback deviates. Route it through a semantic utility (e.g. bg-surface-hover) or reuse StatusHelper's `muted` tone. Minor: functionally correct in both themes.

The toggle's focus indicator uses `focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-indigo-600` rather than the guide's canonically named `focus-visible:ring-2 ring-indigo-600` (§Accessibility baseline / §Buttons). This is a genuine but borderline deviation: a visible indigo focus indicator IS present (no a11y regression), and the outline pattern already pre-exists on main in phases/show.html.erb buttons, so the toggle matches an established in-repo pattern even though it differs from the guide's stated token. The codebase is itself inconsistent (login/form controls use `ring`, phase buttons use `outline`); aligning the toggle to `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-indigo-600` would match the guide's literal convention. Minor.
